classification.stance_proposed: research_blocked; do not advance to a security decision until the critical acceptance tests are met.

one_line_thesis: Mesabi Trust is a contractual iron ore pellet royalty on Cleveland-Cliffs' Northshore (Silver Bay, MN): cash is cyclical commodity + bonus-tier optionality, with ongoing AAA arbitration (commenced Sep 2025) layered on statutory trust simplicity—hold at 8.4% normalized yield for the incumbent royalty sleeve; do not downgrade on depressed Q1 bonus alone.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Mesabi Trust is a contractual pellet royalty on Cliffs' Northshore mine with cyclical commodity exposure and a separate legal recovery option. FY2026 distributions were $1.28 per unit; Q1 2026 bonus was zero despite tonnage more than doubling year over year. Proof-first component base economic value is about $36.40 per unit versus a price near $24.40. At that price, the component-value path implies about 5.9% per year over seven years before distributions. Dhando is partial: no operating debt, but operator concentration and a depleting claim cap upside. Prior review proposed hold for the in...

human_review.model_stance: research_blocked; do not advance to a security decision until the critical acceptance tests are met.

investment_committee.strongest_dissent: The apparent asset value may be illusory because Cliffs controls a finite swing mine and can minimize third-party pricing evidence and production cadence.
unresolved_items: An independently reproducible reserve, production and royalty-tier depletion schedule is absent.

decision.next_action: Close critical evidence gaps before freezing a decision-grade packet.

change_log.author: valuation-contract-v2 migration
change_log.reason: Retire unsupported analyst ranges and make calculation lineage authoritative.
changes: Producing royalty stream changed from provisional $30/$35/$42 to unpriced pending a reserve and royalty distribution schedule.
Standalone concentration reserve changed from additive -$5/-$3/-$1 to an embedded risk control so depletion and downtime cannot be counted twice.
Current arbitration changed from $0/$0/$13 to unpriced; the historical award remains context, not a current payoff estimate.
Trust reserve changed from $1/$2/$3 to calculated $18.341533m / 13.12001m units.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.strongest_dissent: The apparent asset value may be illusory because Cliffs controls a finite swing mine and can minimize third-party pricing evidence and production cadence.
unresolved_items: An independently reproducible reserve, production and royalty-tier depletion schedule is absent.
committee.next_action: Close the committee's primary-evidence blockers before requesting an owner decision.

gaps.question: An independently reproducible reserve, production and royalty-tier depletion schedule is absent.
gaps.evidence_required: Resolve the committee's missing fact with primary evidence and update the affected valuation proof row.
gaps.acceptance_test: The committee item is explicitly closed, sourced, and reflected in the valuation range or documented as immaterial.

method_fit.label: Scarce assets, royalties, and optionality
applicability_tests: The security owns a legally enforceable economic claim on scarce assets or royalties.
Material value is omitted or poorly represented by current GAAP earnings.
Asset quantities, ownership, overlap, timing, and remaining capital can be reconciled.
Comparable values can be adjusted for location, quality, control, liquidity, and realization time.
failure_modes: gross asset value mistaken for shareholder value
operating cash flow and NAV counted twice
remote options treated as contracted cash

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

drivers.label: Trust cash and other net claims

attribution.explanation: Component changes are classified from observable differences in quantity, comparable method, probability, timing, capital, evidence, and schedule scope.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

components.label: Producing royalty and bonus stream
components.assumption_summary: Present value of after-trust royalty distributions under explicit volume, pellet-price, bonus, and discount-rate cases.
components.cross_check: Range must be reconciled to primary filings, normalized economics, and relevant transaction or public-market evidence before IC approval.

components.label: Arbitration and bonus-payment option
components.assumption_summary: Base assigns no value to the undisclosed arbitration. Only a separately evidenced incremental recovery beyond ordinary royalties may enter the high sensitivity; ordinary base and bonus royalties remain in the producing stream.
components.cross_check: Do not promote the high case into base until a primary legal record states a claim, remedy, timing, and collectibility. The 2024 collected award is a closed, non-recurring dispute and is not a comparable current payoff.

components.label: Trust cash and other net claims
components.assumption_summary: Source-locked net trust reserve divided by source-locked units; refresh after every filing.
components.cross_check: Latest disclosed unallocated reserve divided by disclosed units reproduces the component exactly; no unsupported range is added.

components.label: Finite reserve and counterparty concentration reserve
components.assumption_summary: Deduction for depletion, operator concentration, interruption, and terminal-value uncertainty.
components.cross_check: Range must be reconciled to primary filings, normalized economics, and relevant transaction or public-market evidence before IC approval.

economic_value.accounting_reference: Mesabi Trust 2026 Form 10-K and Annual Report, including the audited unallocated reserve bridge.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of MSB, including every identified operating claim, asset, liability, and option.
economic_claim.unit_source: Mesabi Trust 2026 Form 10-K: 13,120,010 units outstanding at April 21, 2026.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating and asset claims are valued once; cash, debt, financing, reserves, and other senior claims are included as separate components or within the stated equity basis.

valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high values incorporate ownership, tax, funding, cycle, and realization adjustments described in the component evidence.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key producing_royalty_stream; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence or through-cycle results support economics below the low case or reveal an omitted claim.

bullets.title: Horizon Kinetics · shorted · MSB · 2023Q2
bullets.summary: Producer: Cleveland Cliffs (CLF), Royalty: Mesabi Trust (MSB); Commodity: CRB Iron Ore 62% FE CFR China Cash ($/t) MARKET COMMENTARY 2nd Quarter 2023 July 2023 © 2023 Horizon Kinetics

timeline.label: Trustees declared $0.05/unit distribution citing lower April royalty and no bonus; July 30 royalty report due.
Q1 2026 royalty report: 938,572 tons shipped, bonus royalty $0 (below $71.70/ton threshold).
Mesabi Trust commenced AAA arbitration vs Northshore/Cliffs on idling and intercompany royalty underpayment.
Cliffs paid $71.185M satisfying prior AAA award (~$5.43/unit); non-recurring distribution.
AAA panel awarded $59.8M damages plus pre-award interest in prior Cliffs royalty dispute.
Northshore idled May 2022–April 2023; subsequent arbitration subject matter for 2025 panel.
Cleveland-Cliffs completed acquisition of remaining Northshore interest; operator concentration increases.
Mesabi Trust formed as passive royalty trust on Northshore Mining pellet leases.

industry.structure: U.S. iron ore pellet royalties are concentrated among a handful of Great Lakes operators; Mesabi Trust is a passive claimant on Cleveland-Cliffs' Northshore swing mine with no operating control.
industry.share_shift: Cliffs/Northshore controls production, shipments, and bonus-tier pricing inputs; trust unitholders are price-takers on operator decisions and pellet market cycles.
industry.trend: Pellet demand tied to domestic EAF steel; bonus tiers make distributions highly variable with realized pellet prices; legal disputes over intercompany pricing add event risk.

top_claims.claim: EPS (basic) growth moved from -96.5% to -70.0% YoY for two consecutive quarters. Trailing-twelve-month growth confirms the same direction. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.
Rare negative YoY quarter(s): YoY growth moved to -12.1% to -70.0% YoY (baseline median +31.5% YoY) for two consecutive quarters. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.

Mesabi Trust is a contractual pellet royalty on Cliffs' Northshore mine with cyclical commodity exposure and a separate legal recovery option. FY2026 distributions were $1.28 per unit; Q1 2026 bonus was zero despite tonnage more than doubling year over year. Proof-first component base economic value is about $36.40 per
At today's price, component proofs imply about **5.9%** per year over seven years on the base economic value path, before counting interim distributions.

risk_claims.claim: EPS (basic) growth moved from -96.5% to -70.0% YoY for two consecutive quarters. Trailing-twelve-month growth confirms the same direction. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.
Rare negative YoY quarter(s): YoY growth moved to -12.1% to -70.0% YoY (baseline median +31.5% YoY) for two consecutive quarters. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.
*Primary risk: Persistently sub-threshold pellet pricing keeps bonus at zero and distributions depressed versus normalized mid-cycle levels.
